title: Tencent Hy3: One Option String From a Window
description: Tencent's 295B MoE joins the clipboard-formatter board on both its paid and free endpoints: a +41-point generational jump over Hunyuan A13B, a static score of 87 tying GLM-4.7 and GPT-OSS 120B — and two startup crashes, each exactly one corrected line away from a working window. Compared against the open MoE cohort and the closed budget tier at the same price.

title: Poolside Laguna XS 2.1: The Coding Agent That Writes AHK v1
description: The board's first Poolside entry: a 33B-A3B coding-agent model that fails the parse gate on both endpoints — not with v2 mistakes, but with a confident v1/JavaScript hybrid dialect. Its static 50 still tops the sub-50B open class and beats closed GPT-5.4 mini by 18 points, while GPT-5.4 nano proves small-and-cheap can ship a running window.

title: AHK-Eval Round 3: Self-Tests, and What Happens When the Harness Is Real
description: Two final harness arms: models writing their own test cases lift the field +5.4 tasks (GPT-5.5 posts the first non-Fable perfect run; one model regresses on its own wrong tests) — and headless Claude Code turns Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 4.6 from 29 and 25 cold into 36/36 and 35/36, at eleven cents a task.

title: AHK-Eval Round 2: What a Coding Harness Is Actually Worth
description: The same 36 tasks and 181 hidden tests, re-run with two harness arms across all 13 models: a rules-card system prompt moves the field +0.4 tasks and hurts as many models as it helps; one round of execution feedback lifts every single model (+3.3 mean) and carries Fable 5 to the suite's first perfect 36/36 — after it wrote its favorite trap yet again.

title: AHK-Eval: 36 Tasks, 181 Hidden Tests, 13 Models
description: A HumanEval-style AHK v2 benchmark: 36 independent functions across six categories and three difficulty tiers, graded against hidden test cases. GPT-5.5 lands one task from perfect; Claude Fable 5 — the model that built the benchmark, run cold over the bare API — takes second at 34/36 and falls into its own favorite trap.

title: Claude Fable 5: Five Entries, Five Windows
description: Claude Fable 5 joins the clipboard-formatter board with five entries under five harness configs — and sweeps both gates: all five parse, all five run. Two ship fully dark 20/20 windows; the bare no-tooling entry is the leanest runner on the board at 40 lines. Updated July 1: a sixth run under the full live harness clears the whole checklist at 98 and takes rank 1 on the board.

title: The Clipboard Toolkit: 11 Coding Models, 12 Transforms
description: A second, deliberately discriminating AHK v2 benchmark: twelve independent clipboard text transforms, graded functionally against hidden test cases. The top-11 coding models spread from 3/12 to 11/12 — with the best logic in the field (Opus 4.8) hung on a crashing GUI.

title: One Prompt, 77 Models: An AHK v2 Clipboard-Formatter Benchmark
description: 77 LLMs answered the same strict AHK v2 prompt, graded on two signals — does it parse, and does it actually run — against the alpha.30 fork, with per-model pages and live OpenRouter sweeps. Only 30 of the 74 that returned code build a working window — Claude Fable 5's five entries all do.

title: Claude Opus 4.8: AHK v2 Output Examples
description: Real, unedited AHK v2 outputs from Claude Opus 4.8 -- idiomatic Map() usage, correct event-callback binding, validated properties, typed error handling, and v2.1 alpha struct overlays. Includes a runnable clipboard-history example.

title: CloudAHK: Remote AHK v2 Execution via REST API
description: A FastAPI service for executing AHK v2 scripts over HTTP with an ANTLR parser endpoint. Includes an AHK v2 client class and template system.

title: AHK v2.1 Alpha Tricks: Unions, Arenas, and Prototype Black Magic
description: Advanced patterns combining struct unions, typed callbacks, DefineProp, and Struct.At -- IEEE 754 dissection, ring buffers, arena allocators, Vec2 math, and dynamic class factories.

title: AHK v2.1-alpha.26: Memory Optimization and Modal Dialog Fixes
description: Struct memory co-allocation for faster construction, array alignment fixes, keyboard shortcuts in modal dialogs, and WM_CLOSE reload.

title: AHK v2.1-alpha.25: Inline Window Options and Stability Fixes
description: The ahk_opt WinTitle criterion for per-call window search options, hotkey behavior fixes, and module system improvements.

title: AHK v2.1-alpha.24: Typed Callbacks and C-Style Unions
description: CallbackCreate with typed parameter arrays for float support, DefineProp Offset for union-like memory overlays, and wildcard re-exports.

title: AHK v2.1-alpha.23: Numeric Types and Struct Arrays
description: Dedicated numeric type classes (Int32, Float32), structured arrays with StructClass[N] syntax, negative indexing, and Size fix.

title: AHK v2.1-alpha.22: The Struct Revolution
description: The native Struct keyword with typed fields, auto-generated .Ptr classes, DefineProp() function, Type() unset, and Win32 API integration.

title: Object-Oriented Programming in LLMs
description: How large language models understand and generate OOP code, patterns they excel at, and leveraging their capabilities for better AHK v2 class design.

title: Building OOP GUIs in AutoHotkey v2
description: A comprehensive guide to creating maintainable, reusable GUI applications using object-oriented principles and component architecture.

title: Creating Dark Mode GUIs in AHK v2
description: Step-by-step tutorial on implementing native dark mode theming using DllCall and Windows API integration.

title: Customizing TapHoldManager.ahk
description: Master the TapHoldManager library for creating sophisticated key combinations with tap, hold, multi-tap, and tap-and-hold behaviors.

title: Prompt Engineering for AHK v2 Code Generation
description: Craft effective prompts to get high-quality AutoHotkey v2 code from LLMs with templates, anti-patterns, and validation techniques.

title: Using AutoHotkey v2 with Coding Agents and LLMs
description: A lightweight workflow for combining AutoHotkey v2 scripts with GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and other coding agents.
